texto: Your old tutor did you a great disservice, Mr. Kynaston. He taught you how to speak, and swoon, and toss your head but he never taught you how to suffer like a woman, or love like a woman. He trapped a man in a woman's form and left you there to die! I always hated you as Desdemona. You never fought! You just died, beautifully. No woman would die like that, no matter how much she loved him. A woman would fight!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stage Beauty: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Your old tutor did you a great disservice, Mr. Kynaston. He taught you how to speak, and swoon, a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, anger, drama pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e drama.

I always hated you as Desdemona. You never fought! You just died, beautifully. No woman would die like that, no matter how much she loved him. A woman would fight!
